risk_statement: Unauthorized staff may access sensitive academic records due to lack of periodic user access review and role recertification.
threat_event: Excessive or outdated access privileges are misused or remain active after role changes.
vulnerability: Access rights are granted through the admin portal but periodic review evidence is not consistently documented.
business_impact: Personal data exposure, unauthorized record changes, compliance issues, and loss of trust in academic service operations.
source_reference: NIST CSF PR.AA; NIST SP 800-53 AC, IA; CIS Controls Account Management; Indonesia PDP Law

risk_statement: Student personal data may be exposed due to excessive admin access and insufficient restriction over sensitive data visibility.
threat_event: An authorized user views or exports personal data beyond their job responsibility.
vulnerability: Role boundaries and data access restrictions are not fully supported by documented review and approval evidence.
business_impact: Potential personal data protection violation, reputational damage, student complaints, and management escalation.
source_reference: NIST CSF GV.OC, PR.DS, PR.AA; NIST SP 800-53 AC, PT; ISO 27001 confidentiality concept; Indonesia PDP Law

risk_statement: Critical academic services may not recover within expected time if backup restoration is not tested and documented.
threat_event: System outage, data corruption, or operational disruption requires restoration from backup.
vulnerability: Backup activity exists, but restore test results and sign-off evidence are unavailable or inconsistent.
business_impact: Extended service downtime, lost operational records, delayed academic services, and weakened recovery confidence.
source_reference: NIST CSF RC.RP, PR.DS; NIST SP 800-53 CP; ISO 27001 availability concept; CIS Data Recovery

risk_statement: System changes may disrupt academic services if approval, testing, and UAT evidence are not consistently documented before release.
threat_event: A configuration or application change introduces errors into registration, admin, or reporting workflows.
vulnerability: Change requests may be approved informally through chat or email without standardized UAT evidence and rollback notes.
business_impact: Service disruption, incorrect processing, user complaints, emergency fixes, and weak audit trail over changes.
source_reference: NIST CSF PR.PS; NIST SP 800-53 CM, SA; COBIT change management concept; CIS Secure Configuration

risk_statement: Security or service incidents may not be escalated consistently due to the absence of a formal severity matrix and escalation SLA.
threat_event: A user reports system access, service outage, or data-related issue that requires coordinated handling.
vulnerability: Incident tickets are logged, but severity classification, response timeline, and escalation ownership are not standardized.
business_impact: Delayed response, inconsistent communication, unresolved incidents, and limited incident trend visibility.
source_reference: NIST CSF RS.MA, RS.CO; NIST SP 800-53 IR; CIS Incident Response Management

risk_statement: Suspicious admin activity may not be detected promptly because privileged activity logs are not reviewed on a defined schedule.
threat_event: An admin account performs unusual data changes, failed login attempts, or excessive record access.
vulnerability: System logs exist, but there is no documented monthly log review process, reviewer, or exception handling evidence.
business_impact: Unauthorized activity remains undetected, investigation becomes difficult, and accountability over privileged access is weakened.
source_reference: NIST CSF DE.CM; NIST SP 800-53 AU; CIS Audit Log Management

risk_statement: Service restoration or issue resolution may be delayed if vendor support obligations and escalation channels are not reviewed periodically.
threat_event: A third-party system or SaaS support dependency affects academic service availability or issue resolution.
vulnerability: Vendor contact, SLA, and escalation expectations are known informally but not reviewed or tracked as GRC evidence.
business_impact: Longer downtime, unclear accountability, delayed incident resolution, and weak third-party oversight.
source_reference: NIST CSF GV.SC; NIST SP 800-53 SR; COBIT supplier/vendor governance concept

risk_statement: Management decisions may rely on inaccurate academic reports if reporting data validation and reconciliation are not performed consistently.
threat_event: Dashboard metrics show incomplete, duplicated, outdated, or inconsistent academic service data.
vulnerability: Reporting dashboard uses operational data, but validation rules and reconciliation checks are not fully documented.
business_impact: Incorrect management reporting, poor operational decisions, rework, and reduced trust in analytics outputs.
source_reference: NIST CSF ID.IM; NIST SP 800-53 SI; COBIT information quality/governance concept

risk_statement: Course registration may become unavailable during peak periods if continuity planning and capacity readiness are not tested.
threat_event: High user traffic or system disruption occurs during course registration period.
vulnerability: Critical registration period is known, but continuity readiness, fallback process, and recovery communication are not tested regularly.
business_impact: Student registration delay, operational backlog, user dissatisfaction, and emergency support pressure.
source_reference: NIST CSF RC.RP, GV.RM; NIST SP 800-53 CP; ISO 27001 availability concept; COBIT continuity concept

risk_statement: Inactive, transferred, or resigned user accounts may remain active due to incomplete offboarding and access revocation tracking.
threat_event: Former or transferred staff account retains access to admin functions after role change or termination.
vulnerability: Account deactivation depends on manual notification and is not fully reconciled against HR or role-change records.
business_impact: Unauthorized access, accountability gaps, personal data exposure, and compliance concerns.
source_reference: NIST CSF PR.AA; NIST SP 800-53 AC, IA; CIS Account Management; Indonesia PDP Law

risk_statement: Payment status information may be inaccurate if data updates are not reconciled and exception cases are not reviewed.
threat_event: Payment status shown to academic staff differs from underlying finance or eligibility record.
vulnerability: Status data is visible in the module, but reconciliation and exception review are not consistently documented.
business_impact: Incorrect service eligibility decisions, student complaints, rework, and weakened data trust.
source_reference: NIST CSF PR.DS, ID.IM; NIST SP 800-53 SI; ISO 27001 integrity concept

risk_statement: Document requests may be closed without sufficient processing evidence or approval trail.
threat_event: A document request is marked complete without evidence of review, approval, or fulfillment.
vulnerability: Request workflow exists, but completion evidence and approval records are not standardized.
business_impact: Disputes over request completion, inconsistent service quality, and weak audit trail for service processing.
source_reference: NIST CSF GV.OV, PR.PS; NIST SP 800-53 PL, CM; COBIT governance and documentation concept

risk_statement: Personal data may be retained longer than necessary if retention and deletion review procedures are not defined.
threat_event: Old student-related records remain stored without business need, retention rationale, or deletion review.
vulnerability: Data retention practices are assumed but not documented or periodically reviewed.
business_impact: Privacy compliance exposure, unnecessary data storage risk, and increased impact if data is exposed.
source_reference: NIST CSF PR.DS, GV.PO; NIST SP 800-53 PT; ISO 27001 confidentiality concept; Indonesia PDP Law

risk_statement: Staff may mishandle credentials or sensitive information due to limited security and privacy awareness training.
threat_event: A staff member shares credentials, mishandles student data, or falls for social engineering.
vulnerability: Security awareness activities are not consistently scheduled, tracked, or evidenced.
business_impact: Credential compromise, data exposure, unauthorized access, and increased operational security risk.
source_reference: NIST CSF PR.AT, GV.RR; NIST SP 800-53 AT; CIS Security Awareness and Skills Training

risk_statement: Known vulnerabilities in supporting systems or third-party components may remain unresolved if vulnerability tracking is not maintained.
threat_event: A known vulnerability affects database, reporting, or vendor-supported components.
vulnerability: No centralized vulnerability backlog or documented remediation tracking is maintained for in-scope assets.
business_impact: Increased exposure to exploitation, service disruption, data compromise, and delayed remediation accountability.
source_reference: NIST CSF ID.RA, PR.PS; NIST SP 800-53 RA, SI; CIS Continuous Vulnerability Management; optional NVD reference
